# services.authelia

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.authelia`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.authelia.instances` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | Multi-domain protection currently requires multiple instances of Authelia. If you don't require multiple instances of Authelia you can define just the one. <https://www.authelia.com/roadmap/active/multi-domain-protection/> |
| `services.authelia.instances.<name>.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Authelia instance. |
| `services.authelia.instances.<name>.environmentVariables` | `attribute set of string` | Additional environment variables to provide to authelia. If you are providing secrets please consider the options under {option}`services.authelia.<instance>.secrets` or make sure you use the `_FILE` suffix. If you provide the raw secret rather than the location of a secret file that secret will be preserved in the nix store. For more details: <https://www.authelia.com/configuration/methods/secrets/> |
| `services.authelia.instances.<name>.group` | `string` | The name of the group for this authelia instance. |
| `services.authelia.instances.<name>.name` | `string` | Name is used as a suffix for the service name, user, and group. By default it takes the value you use for `<instance>` in: {option}`services.authelia.instances.<instance>` When set to the empty string `""`, the service name, user, and group will be just `authelia` without a suffix. |
| `services.authelia.instances.<name>.package` | `package` | The authelia package to use. |
| `services.authelia.instances.<name>.secrets` | `submodule` | It is recommended you keep your secrets separate from the configuration. It's especially important to keep the raw secrets out of your nix configuration, as the values will be preserved in your nix store. This attribute allows you to configure the location of secret files to be loaded at runtime. <https://www.authelia.com/configuration/methods/secrets/> |
| `services.authelia.instances.<name>.secrets.jwtSecretFile` | `null or absolute path` | Path to your JWT secret used during identity verificaton. |
| `services.authelia.instances.<name>.secrets.manual` | `boolean` | Configuring authelia's secret files via the secrets attribute set is intended to be convenient and help catch cases where values are required to run at all. If a user wants to set these values themselves and bypass the validation they can set this value to true. |
| `services.authelia.instances.<name>.secrets.oidcHmacSecretFile` | `null or absolute path` | Path to your HMAC secret used to sign OIDC JWTs. |
| `services.authelia.instances.<name>.secrets.oidcIssuerPrivateKeyFile` | `null or absolute path` | Path to your private key file used to encrypt OIDC JWTs. |
| `services.authelia.instances.<name>.secrets.sessionSecretFile` | `null or absolute path` | Path to your session secret. Only used when redis is used as session storage. |
| `services.authelia.instances.<name>.secrets.storageEncryptionKeyFile` | `null or absolute path` | Path to your storage encryption key. |
| `services.authelia.instances.<name>.settings` | `open submodule of (YAML 1.1 value)` | Your Authelia config.yml as a Nix attribute set. There are several values that are defined and documented in nix such as `default_2fa_method`, but additional items can also be included. <https://github.com/authelia/authelia/blob/master/config.template.yml> |
| `services.authelia.instances.<name>.settings.default_2fa_method` | `one of "", "totp", "webauthn", "mobile_push"` | Default 2FA method for new users and fallback for preferred but disabled methods. |
| `services.authelia.instances.<name>.settings.log.file_path` | `null or absolute path` | File path where the logs will be written. If not set logs are written to stdout. |
| `services.authelia.instances.<name>.settings.log.format` | `one of "json", "text"` | Format the logs are written as. |
| `services.authelia.instances.<name>.settings.log.keep_stdout` | `boolean` | Whether to also log to stdout when a `file_path` is defined. |
| `services.authelia.instances.<name>.settings.log.level` | `one of "trace", "debug", "info", "warn", "error"` | Level of verbosity for logs. |
| `services.authelia.instances.<name>.settings.server.address` | `string` | The address to listen on. |
| `services.authelia.instances.<name>.settings.telemetry.metrics.address` | `string` | The address to listen on for metrics. This should be on a different port to the main `server.port` value. |
| `services.authelia.instances.<name>.settings.telemetry.metrics.enabled` | `boolean` | Enable Metrics. |
| `services.authelia.instances.<name>.settings.theme` | `one of "light", "dark", "grey", "auto"` | The theme to display. |
| `services.authelia.instances.<name>.settingsFiles` | `list of absolute path` | Here you can provide authelia with configuration files or directories. It is possible to give authelia multiple files and use the nix generated configuration file set via {option}`services.authelia.<instance>.settings`. |
| `services.authelia.instances.<name>.user` | `string` | The name of the user for this authelia instance. |
